title: FMLA Tracking Mistakes Cost Thousands in DOL Fines
yt_description:
Sticky note FMLA tracking stops working the day you miscalculate someone's twelve weeks. I've seen the Department of Labor fines—they're not small. If you're managing leave requests manually right now, you need automated tracking that calculates rolling twelve-month periods correctly.

Manual FMLA tracking is a ticking time bomb for US small businesses. One miscalculation in those rolling twelve-month periods and you're facing hefty Department of Labor penalties. The complexity of tracking intermittent leave, calculating available time, and managing overlapping leave types makes spreadsheets and sticky notes practically useless.

Modern leave management software automates these calculations, ensures compliance with federal and state regulations, and provides the documentation you need during audits. Features like automated notifications, employee self-service portals, and integration with payroll systems eliminate the guesswork that leads to costly violations.

Don't wait for a DOL audit to discover your tracking system failed. Get matched with the right leave management solution for your business size and industry needs.
